summary: Iteration 106 — Audit Log CSV Export backend tests. All 23 pytest cases for GET /api/admin/access-logs/export-csv pass (RBAC, headers, filename, filters, date-range, 400 validation, audit-of-the-audit, row cap). Extended list endpoint date filters also verified. Regression: iteration_103 / 104 / 105 all still green (50 passed, 2 skipped — nurse account absent).
critical_code_review_comments: resource_type for procedure GETs is logged as 'procedure' (singular) while many endpoints elsewhere use plural collection names — consistency cleanup would help audit-log filtering UX.

audit_export self-log currently records row_count computed from buf.getvalue().count('\n') - 1 which is fine for simple values, but any field containing a literal newline (user_agent, extra JSON) would make row_count diverge from real rows. Consider counting data rows at the source (len(rows_written)).

The CSV endpoint does not paginate beyond the 10k cap — if/when access_logs grow past 10k for a filter window, the silent truncation has no indicator (no header, no warning row). Recommend adding an 'X-Truncated: true' response header or a trailing comment row when cap is hit.
CSV 'extra' column serializes via json.dumps without stripping embedded commas/quotes — csv.writer handles escaping, so this is safe; confirmed during test_parse.
test_credentials: Used seeded admin Abhijit.patil / Student Gaurav.pandey / Supervisor Paresh.gandhi / Nurse nurse.1@dental.edu from /app/memory/test_credentials.md (nurse account exists and works).
seed_data_creation: Did not create new users. Generated a fresh failed-login row (identifier iter106_ghost_<ts>) and reused procedure 69cf9bdfdafb502718057bcd to produce a procedure_view audit row inside the seeded_audit fixture.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iteration 106 test file lives at /app/backend/tests/test_audit_log_csv_export_iteration106.py. Header row order assumed by UI export is hard-coded in EXPECTED_HEADER (11 cols). Note: resource_type for procedure_view is singular ('procedure'), not 'procedures'. The audit_export self-log test relies on incremental count, so any concurrent run hitting /admin/access-logs/export-csv during the 2s window would make it flaky — run tests serially.
rca of the issue: No issues found. Two initial test-script problems were self-corrected: (1) used wrong plural resource_type 'procedures' in filter assertion (backend uses 'procedure'); (2) naive-vs-aware datetime subtraction in freshness check — fixed by normalising parsed datetime to UTC tz-aware.
